observations.summary: The physical and emotional struggle of climbing the rope with burning grips and aching arms parallels Dan's internal struggle with doubt and the acceptance of brokenness, symbolizing the challenge of overcoming personal barriers and familial legacies.

source_evidence.quote: He stood at the base of a rope—thick, rough-woven hemp, blood-warm and slick with something between sweat and oil, vibrating with the hum of the emptiness it pierced. Each grip burned with tiny splinters. He climbed. The air grew thin, then cold, then ringing-silent. A moment of doubt arrived and lodged in him like a stone: some barriers are meant to stand. Some fathers are meant to stay broken. He climbed through it. His arms ached and his breath came in sharp urgent gasps and he climbed through that too, until his hand found an edge—solid, unmoving—and he pulled himself up and over.

observations.target_hint: This physical ascent echoes Dan's broader spiritual and psychological ascent throughout the narrative, where he confronts internal and external obstacles, including his relationship with his father and his own doubts.
